sections.paragraph:
σύμφορος, ον (συμφέρω) beneficial, advantageous, profitable (Hes., Hdt. et al.; ins; POxy 1676, 25 τὸ σύνφορόν σοι ποίει; 2 Macc 4:5; Just., D. 8, 1) τινί to or for someone Hv 1, 3, 3; 5:5; Hs 6, 1, 3; 6, 5, 7; 7:5. Comp. (Epict. 1, 28, 7; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 294) συμφορώτερόν ἐστι w. inf. foll. Hm 6, 1, 4 (συμφερώτερον Joly, s. his app.).—Subst. τὸ σύμφορον benefit, advantage (Thu. 5, 98. The pl. τὰ σύμφορα is more freq. Soph. et al.) τό τινος σύμφορον 1 Cor 7:35; 10:33.—Frisk s.v. φέρω B 3. M-M. TW.

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Hv Hv = Visions

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

Hm Hm = Mandates

Joly Joly = Hermas, Le Pasteur; s. under H—List 1

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

  1. pl. = plural

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Frisk Frisk = HF., Griechisches etymologisches Wörterbuch—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 960
